sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 6.19 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.6)

statement.latex: Let$R$be a nilpotent associative ring. Are the following two statements for a subgroup$H$of the adjoint group of$R$always equivalent: \ 1)$H$is a normal subgroup; \ 2)$H$is an ideal of the groupoid$R$with respect to Lie multiplication? { V.\,M.\,Levchuk} Not always.
